category: Art, Design, Architecture and Technical Productions
description: I was an intern in the Education Department of the Bellevue Arts Museum. I helped with the planning and production of Education programming, which included Get Crafty from Home as well as the Teen Arts Council. I developed weekly project ideas for the Get Crafty with BAM program, including creating online content and Bellevue School District art kits. I also research project ideas for Free Family Day To-Go programs and helped manage budgeting. I also was part of a team that developed a virtual program for teen artists throughout the Greater Seattle Area. Overall, I gained a lot of experience in museum education, family programs, arts learning, and public programming.
unique_experiences: • Education / Crafting: Every week, I wrote instructions for "Get Crafty from Home" with my mentor. It was really helpful to navigate the best way to phrase a tricky step or describe a complicated craft together. I will definitely be applying these skills I acquired to other things. • Graphic Design: I loved designing the craft instructions, scavenger hunts, promotional posters, and more! I like the challenge of being given a color scheme and/or a set theme and finding new ways to make the design pop each subsequent time. • Marketing: I was in charge of the Teen Arts Council social media, so planning the days on which I was supposed to create and post content was definitely good for practicing organization.
surprised: The flexibility and the creativity. Every member of the Education Team was so welcoming and really made me feel at home as an intern who was still learning the ropes. Teamwork makes the dream work! Consequently, the crafts we came up with for many, many events were really quite cool and innovative for kids to explore and experiment with.
takeaways: The people are always going to be the best part of any experience! I formed really close friendships with the members of the Education Team throughout this past year, and I'll definitely be keeping in touch with them!

category: Art, Design, Architecture and Technical Productions
description: GLY is a contracting firm, meaning that they help construct buildings that have already been designed by architects. Although virtual this unique blend of construction management and architecture provides an entirely new look at the way both fields interact. Due to the nature of the project that I was helping with and the contracting industry, I helped with nearly every task imaginable, adding to the aspects of the industry that I was exposed to.
unique_experiences: • Talking with subcontractor "leaders" to gain specialized knowledge on different fields • Access to countless construction documents regarding the project • Listening in on meetings with subcontractors • Creating actual construction documents to be used in the final project
surprised: The scope of the effect of the work I was doing definitely surprised me, especially as an intern – the documents that I worked on day in and day out were constantly used in some form or another by actual, working adults in the professional field.
takeaways: I would definitely say the unique look that I got at a STEM field that I was interested in was the greatest takeaway – the hands-on-ness of the project, and the knowledge that what I was working on actually mattered in the work field, really allowed me to get the best look possible into both architecture and contracting/construction management.
mentor_name: n/a This internship is not available

category: Art, Design, Architecture and Technical Productions
description: Internship work for Loopie Laundry in Seattle, WA. Worked in pre-production and post- production for a documentary about the CEO of the company. The film will follow John as he goes from bussing tables at a restaurant to founding a successful start-up. Pre- production involved research on neorealist films, brainstorming sessions, timeline creation, storyboard creation, script creation, and revision. Post-production involved the organization of digital media and the assembly/editing of footage.
unique_experiences: • Working with many hours of existing footage • Working within the constraints of the neorealist genre • Working so closely with the CEO of a start-up
surprised: I am still amazed at the volume and variety of footage that John was able to capture while starting his company. The existence of this footage practically justifies the creation of a documentary about the creation of the company.
takeaways: This was my first time working on a film where I was not the creative director of the project. I learned that it is of paramount importance to have a strong sense of mutual understanding between myself and the creative director of the project. Without this understanding, the visions of the director and editor can at times be contradictory, creating delays in production.

category: Art, Design, Architecture and Technical Productions
description: Aventure Trading Inc. is a fintech startup that combines quantitative and fundamental investing to provide the average investor Wall Street trading strategies. UI/UX Design serves as a connection between an app user and a company, prioritizing ease of access for the customer while reflecting the style and brand of the company simultaneously. At Aventure, I was able to be the UI/UX Designer for their proto-type app in which I was given reign to explore design elements (within approved drafts) to create a more unique and interesting user experience.
unique_experiences: • Exploration of design elements to improve user experience • Prototyping & leading design at Aventure • Learning InVision, a popular UI/UX program
surprised: I am surprised at the fast-paced and independent nature of a fintech startup has; It teaches you to become more adapt at problem solving and improving communication skills.
takeaways: My greatest takeaway from my internship experience is that learning new ideas/concepts/programs is best when you dive headfirst!

category: Art, Design, Architecture and Technical Productions
description: My official intern title was "Digital Media Intern", but I ended up doing a variety of things. This included graphic design, digital illustration, social media management/marketing, website design (UI/UX), and some sales work (surveys and cold calls). I worked independently on my own time and also had a few scheduled meetings every week. Aakanksha Deb, Tanvi Adhikari, and I worked in a pod together, which was really fun! We also worked closely with a few other employees besides our mentor.
unique_experiences: • Redesigning their investor pitch deck with a design mentor • Helping the design team redesign one of the company websites • Independently designing a monthly newsletter
surprised: I was actually really surprised by how my role in the company changed over the course of the internship. With startups, you naturally have people coming and going pretty often. At one point, there were no designers at all in the company, so I was only doing digital illustration, social media, and sales work. A few months later, a designer joined and that opened the door for me to get more involved with design as their mentee.
takeaways: Staying organized is incredibly important, especially when you have to work from home. Planned meetings typically go a lot faster and are more productive than unplanned ones. Another pro-tip: taking notes during meetings when necessary, because sometimes you just forget the specifics of what people asked you to do. "Organizing" my time (aka having a schedule) was also helpful. There were scheduled days where I always did my internship work, and that made it a lot easier for me to get everything done.

category: Art, Design, Architecture, and Technical Productions
organization: WISER (Women in Surgery at Emory Residencies) Podcast
location: Remote only (podcast is based in Atlanta, Georgia)
description: The WISER Podcast is a podcast that interviews women in different fields of surgery and talks about their experiences in them. It often focuses on implicit/explicit biases against women in surgery as well as against other communities (LGBTQ, Black, International). Episodes cover topics such as pregnancy in residency, female leadership in surgery, etc. As their art/merchandise intern, I was given a lot of freedom in terms of what I wanted the episode covers to look like and collaborated on merchandise design.
unique_experiences: • Designed covers for each episode that were published on Spotify/Apple Podcasts • Created animations for episodes to be published on social media • Produced merchandise through screen printing/machine embroidery – packaged and shipped all products. • Created other social media posts – videos/graphics • Sat through podcast recordings with leading surgeons
surprised: Sitting through podcast recordings and listening to recordings before they are condensed was incredibly fascinating. Each woman I met had amazing stories to tell and shows the hard work and passion they have for their career.
takeaways: This internship experience allowed me to learn how to animate and taught me various other new skills such as using descript to edit a podcast. However, my greatest takeaway was truly being inspired by the incredible women I met and listened about.

category: Art, Design, Architecture and Technical Productions
description: I was a teaching assistant for Mrs. Leblanc, who taught the courses Art Exploration 3D (middle school) and 2D Art & Design (high school).
unique_experiences: • Created worksheets, tutorials, and supplemental materials (PowerPoints) • Presented live art demonstrations using Autodesk SketchBook • Drew examples for assignments • Connected with students one-on-one about assignments and art in general • Helped out with lesson planning
surprised: How much I could actually influence, inspire, and get to know the students—especially in middle school! Even in a mostly virtual setting, it surprised me how receptive some students were to the projects and lessons, and some even reached out to me on their own to talk about art. This was probably my favorite part of the internship because it was really fulfilling to encourage students to make more art and see them grow as artists.
takeaways: Having a mentor figure in your life is incredibly impactful and important. I was on both sides of this! Mrs. Leblanc had a lot of wisdom to share as an artist and was very supportive of my career goals in the arts, and at the same time I felt that I could act as a mentor and inspire a lot of the younger students to keep creating.

description: My role was mainly a marketing intern for a peer-to-peer laundry service. I assisted with social media by helping create a regular posting schedule on Instagram and LinkedIn. I also created a customer profile, which highlights Loopie's current and prospective customers with potential marketing templates. I assisted the sales team in cold calling and emailing potential commercial customers for a customer outreach project. I worked with the washer acquisition team to create a monthly newsletter that provides important updates to the employees.
unique_experiences: My mentor was very flexible, and I was able to take part in multiple projects outside of just marketing. The company is very collaborative so there are lots of opportunities to work with different teams and everyone is very friendly.
surprised: I was surprised by how flexible my mentor was and his willingness to changing tasks and starting new projects that I was more interested in. My mentor's biggest goal was to give me a lot of work experience with different types of projects to help me build my resume and explore my passions.
takeaways: This was my first time working in a real business environment, and although it was remote, I was able to explore different projects and see what I enjoyed and what I was skilled in. It is important to communicate with your mentor and don't be afraid to negotiate projects or deadlines.

description: Contact updating and uploading on the company database, database cleanup, designing and formatting pitches for multiple different clients and candidates. Shadowing employees on calls, market research on clients and candidates. My time working remotely was mostly completing assignments given to me, but I also got to see how the employees worked with each other and with candidates. My responsibilities would constantly change as we received new job orders and demand shifted around the company.
unique_experiences: • Direct window into the inner workings of the recruiting industry • Training and constant work in a common recruiting database software • Writing and Formatting short pitches and descriptions of client companies. • Access to large amounts of notes and logs about the process of recruiting.
surprised: I was most surprised about how many failed deals it takes to complete even one successful one. This career requires a lot of persistence and mental fortitude to persevere through failure.
takeaways: How companies look at their candidates for hire and how important first impressions and interactions are during interviews. This internship gave me a direct window of observation into the process of hiring, where I learned a lot of lessons about what to do and what not to do, which will help me later in life.

description: Being a program management intern at Microsoft deals with a merger of user experience/interface design and software development. I worked on Microsoft Teams devices where I created product specifications for features that I thought were useful to add to Teams devices. My mentor was very accommodating, and I got to work with other design leads and senior researchers. Program management was much less computer science, more so the planning process of software engineering.
unique_experiences: • Experience reviewing and commenting Figmas (a design software) • Design features based on ideas that you have and competitor analysis • Primary research with live customer and test user interviews for test products • Secondary research with the massive Microsoft Library • Product specification writing and deck (presentation) work for MS leads • Resource allocation planning and reviews for each quarter
surprised: I was surprised with how organic the idea develop process at major software companies can be. It was also an experience to learn the logistical load program managers deal with to make their ideas come into fruition.
takeaways: I learned a lot about taking the business and engineering principles we learn in some classes more seriously and at much higher stakes. It is impassioning to know that when you have an issue a product, you can create a product spec to solve it and work with people to implement that solution.

description: Bond Intelligence is a start-up which predicts the prices of the bond market and establishes trust and transparency between the city government and its people through OpenEXA – sharing of financial, environmental, municipal, etc. information to the public. My tasks included creating weekly infographic posts and managing the social media accounts (Instagram, Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, etc).
unique_experiences: I had the freedom to work on whatever interested me and was easily able to switch to different tasks if I felt bored.
surprised: How easy it was to talk to your mentors and ask for help!
takeaways: I was able to learn how a professional work environment works and made many valuable connections.

description: Bond Intelligence is a company that provides software that makes investing in bonds simpler. Bond Intelligence also has a project named OpenEXA which works BTB as well as BTC in providing people a better insight into their own local government. My time there allowed me to very closely experience what it is like to work in a professional environment. The hours there are very flexible and will work around your schedule.
unique_experiences: • Reach out and experience one to one meetings with local governments • Experience in marketing targeted towards both companies and the general public
surprised: I am surprised by how supportive and helpful my mentors were.
takeaways: Business to business marketing is not something me or a lot of my other interns had experience in. Being able to experience this and see how the meetings go between businesses is very interesting as there is a very fine balance between friendliness as well as business matters.

description: Bond Intelligence is a University of Washington startup company that strives to help fixed income professionals find opportunities in the marketplace. Our product does everything from running rigorous quantitative analysis in ML scenario-based predictions with real-time data to automating customized programs based on the customer's unique investment strategy.
unique_experiences: • As a smaller start-up business, my mentors were very flexible with scheduling one-on-one or team meetings and accommodations were made if I had personal scheduling conflicts. • Taking the lead in starting and managing larger projects • Some aspects of graphic design, social media presence and management
surprised: I am most surprised and grateful for the flexibility of my mentors when necessary.
takeaways: This was my first marketing internship experience, and my greatest takeaway is learning how to adjust to abrupt changes in a workplace environment. Over the course of my internship, Bond Intelligence shifted its focus from a product aimed towards municipalities to one aimed towards individual consumers. Learning to adjust to a fairly significant change in a short period of time was challenging but very valuable experience.

description: Loopie Laundry is a peer-to-peer laundry service that connects those who don't have time to do their laundry with those who want to earn money by washing and drying laundry at their home. Loopie serves other businesses, such as salons and spas, as well as regular people. At Loopie, I worked on social media management (mostly Instagram), creating materials for Loopie employees such as Washer Newsletters, and some sales outreach.
unique_experiences: • Cold calling and emailing potential customers, since I had never done this before • Posting and engaging with followers on a Loopie's social media • Hosting events such as happy hour calls to increase engagement between employees in a virtual environment
surprised: How fast Loopie was able to expand in the time I worked there. Since beginning my internship, Loopie established networks in Portland and Denver in addition to their original network in Seattle. They are also beginning to offer services in Austin in July.
takeaways: This was my first time working at a company and applying the business information I learned at school to an actual business. I learned that small start up environments move quickly and are very flexible. Instead of having strong and extremely defined roles, employees just work together to finish tasks. This flexibility was something I really enjoyed and one of my main takeaways was that I'd like to find a similar environment when I start working post high school.

description: I focused on gaining an understanding of what management consulting is and what day to day work looks like. By working with some amazing mentors and really cool people, I worked on projects from clients like Ben's Fund, Treehouse4Kids, Seattle Seahawks, etc., gaining some real-world insight into how projects and initiatives are accomplished.
unique_experiences: Conducted research for Ben's Fund, a Seattle non-profit for autism. Surveyed, analyzed, and developed a list of service providers (therapy clinics, summer camps, law firms, etc.) that enabled Ben's Fund to connect the autism community to valuable resources. My recommendations helped connect local autism community to 300+ service providers.
surprised: The pacing of the work environment. Things move very quickly in a work environment and no one is going to baby you. If you don't meet a deadline it falls on you and it will be held against you. Consistency is very important, especially when you are first starting out at a company.
takeaways: I feel much more prepared for a real work environment, whether that be an internship in college or my first job out of college. Working in consulting, I was able to gauge if this is something I want to do for the rest of my life and was very pleased to find that not only could I accomplish the work, I also enjoyed it.

description: Aynuk is a startup developing software for augmented reality solutions in fields such as manufacturing, field service and solar installation. At Aynuk I did market research, competitor analysis and formulated social media strategies for the team.
unique_experiences: • Experience in a growing field • Flexible hours
surprised: I was surprised at the amount of companies already in the industry, because Aynuk is a startup.
takeaways: Active communication is very important and sometimes you have to work without having full guidance and learning to make your own decisions without being told exactly what to do is important.

description: As an intern at Bellevue Badminton Club, I helped in a variety of tasks related to marketing and administration with the general goal of promoting the growth of the club.
unique_experiences: • Designing fliers to market and advertise club apparel • Creating and designing new website • Reaching out to local businesses to help advertise the club o Making deals to get discounts for members to promote both businesses • Calling parents to inform them about summer camp opportunities
surprised: I was most surprised by the sheer amount and variety of operations that a small business is involved with.
takeaways: My greatest takeaway from this experience is that I need to advocate for what I need or want. I've learned that people are busy and they don't always remember to respond or have the time to do things for you. Because of this, I found that it is ok to remind people if I need something done and that I shouldn't be afraid to speak up.

description: Ocean Drive Studios is a new indie game studio looking to produce and market their first TRPG game Lost Eidolons. As an intern, I performed research to help start their kickstarter by gaining valuable feedback from kickstarters ran by other game franchises. I also gave miscellaneous feedback by playtesting and reviewing concepts the team were creating. As a startup, I felt like my voice was heard throughout the process and were flexible with my work schedule in senior year.
unique_experiences: Researching existing TRPG competitors' games by actually playing them - Creating one massive spreadsheet and performing comprehensive research (very satisfying) - Summarizing research in executive summaries and offering concrete advice/steps. - Being able to be one of the few early testers of the game
surprised: How a small informal team still created an incredible product that gained way more attention than a indie company of their size should.
takeaways: The importance of condensing the hours of work you put into research into something concise and clear to take away and actually use.

description: I served as the marketing manager at Haiku Buffet, where I was responsible for everything from communicating with other platforms to managing the social media platforms to executing marketing campaigns.
unique_experiences: • Learning to take photos of foods for Instagram • SEO for delivery platforms • Social media engagement
surprised: I was surprised at the results of the marketing campaigns since they were usually different from my predictions.
takeaways: My greatest takeaway is the hands-on factor of the internship. Simulated marketing campaigns and real life marketing campaigns are very different, and actually getting to implement them pushed me to grow a lot.

description: Goodwell is a small e-commerce business working to form connections between suppliers and consumers. It currently acts as a service that builds websites connecting suppliers to a larger company but plans to create an app that suppliers can use to both monitor business data and create websites of their own.
unique_experiences: • Making period commission reports • Reviewing sites in development for design issues and web accessibility • Logging and monitoring website performance metrics, shadowing security analyst • Managing process/procedure documents and shared document sites
surprised: I was most surprised by how much trust and openness there is involved with a small business. My work was flexible, and I learned a lot about business from the weekly meetings summarizing each team's work and the business plan for the company.
takeaways: The benefit of an internship is that you are there to not only work, but also to learn. At times, when there are not many tasks assigned to you, you can ask other teams how you might be able to help them, and if there is any material they would recommend you learn so that you would be able to do so.

description: The Bellevue Chamber of Commerce is responsible for connecting different businesses in Bellevue, providing them opportunities to network and discuss a variety of issue topics. My time there provided me with hands on experience with a variety of different tasks, such as attending and taking minutes for the Policy Council, drafting documents utilized for the Annual Gala preparation meetings, as well as reaching out to members to help coordinate Third Thursday Deals.
unique_experiences: • Valuable experience in marketing and communications: market research, marketing on different news sites, and note-taking for a variety of different projects • Outreach opportunities, ranging from membership surveys and deals, YEA! application announcements, etc. • Networking opportunities with a variety of different business leaders and Chamber staff
surprised: I am surprised with how flexible the Bellevue of Chamber is. They are very understanding of your needs and are very open to providing a variety of tasks depending on your interests. If you want to try a different variety of work, simply reach out and they will try to make that happen.
takeaways: My biggest takeaway from the internship experience is that I became more aware of how different businesses interact through the Bellevue Chamber of Commerce.

description: Clouidx is a Cloud Solutions Integrator that specializes in delivering advanced analytics solutions, application development and E2E solution delivery for Ips. During my time with Cloudix I worked on researching and executing a new technology discovery project. This new technology is being used to augment an already existing Tele-Health Solution. My time with Cloudix allowed me to explore digital application wireframing, work one-on-one with app developers, and construct research reports on existing technologies and software.
unique_experiences: • Prototyping wireframe simulations for user stories • Developing application design with app developers • Learning about Azure cloud infrastructure and Azure Development Operations
surprised: I was surprised with the with how a smaller company like Cloudix worked efficiently and effectively to meet their various software development solution goals.
takeaways: Going into this opportunity, I had a limited understanding of the technologies and the process used for application development. To gain knowledge and be productive, I conducted independent research on the technologies. This helped me understand the requirements and complex areas where I needed additional support. I came up to speed on the core product manager responsibilities and drafted a plan with my mentor's support.

description: Marketing Internship: doing whatever needs to be done in terms of marketing. I created social media posts, kept the social media running (engaged with other accounts, updated anything, etc.), wrote blog posts, made animations, set up the Facebook Shop, etc. A large part of the job was creating Facebook ad campaigns, running them, and providing feedback/suggestions.
unique_experiences: • Learning the ins and outs of everything and anything related to Facebook (the interface is horrible, and a lot of time was spent just figuring things out) • Seeing how products are released and advertised at a small business. The entire company has less than 20 people working full time and I learned a lot about how they operate.
surprised: The inconsistency in the workload. Some weeks I had a lot of work and other weeks I didn't. To meet the required hours, some weeks I created tasks for myself to do. Since the Instagram was previously neglected, this was often in the form of Instagram posts. I thought this would be an internship where I was just doing tasks, but sometimes I had to pitch ideas and that was fine.
takeaways: You'll probably be doing the same thing for multiple weeks or doing something new every week. Just be flexible and willing to learn. Additionally, besides gaining some form of experience, you are also there to learn about the position, the process, and the company. There's nothing wrong with asking questions or suggesting new things.

description: Bond Intelligence has 2 major products that I've worked on in my time as an intern. Firstly, the OpenEXA application is a platform for government data transparency that was aimed towards citizens to learn more about their municipalities and for municipalities to provide open data visualization. The second product from Bond Intelligence is a fixed income trading platform that offers research and analysis functions for asset managers and fixed income traders in the financial sector. As an intern, I had incredible flexibility in designing and implementing marketing strategies from working on content marketing with Adobe software, to social media, and even interacting with municipalities in sales.
unique_experiences: • Learning about video and general content creation with Adobe Premier, Illustrator, and Spark • Finding ways to connect features from the platforms to benefits for consumers through an overarching story • Building a social media presence from the ground up
surprised: The willingness of start-ups and small companies to try new things and experiment. As interns we were given a lot of trust and we were always seen as equal contributors to the project. They valued our inputs as their own.
takeaways: While most of the intern base at Bond Intelligence was mostly focused on the technical aspect. As a marketing intern, I learned a lot about communicating a product through different lenses. Every user has a unique priority and as a marketer, I need to fulfill their priority as best as possible.

description: Bathing Beauteas is a small, local women-owned start-up based in Seattle that sells handmade tea baths, hand lettered cards, and more. As an intern, my job was primarily in the area of digital marketing and product design. I designed instruction cards that are sold alongside the tea baths, and also strategized ways to promote BB on social media platforms. However, my mentors were open to my interests and shaped the internship to what I wanted to learn about running a small business.
unique_experiences: • I worked with several other college interns (primarily UW) and were able to consult them on topics both related and unrelated to BB. • Though we were unable to this year, BB typically partners with nonprofit organizations like Mary's Place by hosting events to teach participants about entrepreneurship and the importance of self- care. • Experience with digital design software like Procreate, AutoCAD, and Photoshop.
surprised: Because BB is a small business, I was able to clearly see the impact of my work there, whether it was by seeing the products I helped design on the market, or seeing sales increase after a marketing endeavor I contributed to.
takeaways: My mentors were extremely flexible and open to me experiencing various aspects of running a small business based on what I wanted to do. I chose to focus on user-experience, product, and visual design, through which I learned and grew to appreciate that there is a lot of intention behind every choice made in a business, from the colors used in an ad to the ingredients used in the product.

description: Taiyo Pacific Partners is a hedge fund sponsor that primarily operates in Japan, working with Japanese corporations. TPP provides investment and advice to corporations' management to bolster the corporation's performance. As an intern, I got to do preliminary research on topics, translate documents, and create a presentation based on my research.
unique_experiences: ● Valuable experience in financial research ● Working with highly qualified professionals, most graduating from top colleges such as Harvard and M.I.T.
surprised: I was surprised at the advice provided towards the managerial employees of client corporations. I thought it would present concepts at a higher level; however, It was presented in an extremely easy to understand and simple way.
takeaways: This was my first time experiencing a professional work environment, and working with coworkers. My greatest takeaway was the importance of communication in such an environment. Clarifying any unclear points is vital, especially since unlike at school, instructions are often not clear. However I also learned that if it is evident that I am supposed to use my own discretion, my own decision making skills are important..

description: • Worked on a neural network model deployment through various platforms, including Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud • Collaborated with marketing to design a system to track email engagement and identify interested customers for follow-up emails. • Developed interactive end-user dashboards in Looker. • Worked in small groups with other interns. • Variety of projects to get involved with but the interns were generally divided into marketing or technology.
unique_experiences: • There was a lot of flexibility in what projects you worked on, if you had an idea of how to approach a problem you had the freedom to explore that. • Participating in business to business and investor meetings.
surprised: I'm still surprised at the opportunities this internship had, we are consistently asked to participate in important meetings with other companies and with investors.
takeaways: Getting in a startup environment has been a great experience, it gave me the chance to work on and gain experience with a wide variety of technologies, while learning how to work effectively while entirely remote.

description: Bond Intelligence is a small business that uses data science and machine learning to offer bond traders a comprehensive resource of bond predictions and other data. There was also the OpenExa project, which was a service primarily offered to municipal governments seeking to establish more transparency with their citizens; the service would condense the many complicated statistics from the government's Comprehensive Annual Financial report (CAFR) into easy-to-understand visuals.
unique_experiences: • Extensive Work with SQL and database management • Developing of machine learning models • Front-end design (working within the Looker platform to create these UIs called dashboards, which would offer users an interactive way to look through data)
surprised: How flexible some small businesses are – firstly, this internship opportunity had flexible hours. Furthermore, I worked on a wide variety of projects throughout my time here depending on what the main focus at the time was.
takeaways: The effectiveness of collaboration – very daunting tasks can often be broken down into smaller tasks and divided up among a group, and then it is not only done very effectively, it will also be done very efficiently.

description: AaronSec provides vulnerability hunting and security consulting services for prospective companies who need comprehensive reviews of their security posture. As a small business, I was able to work directly with my mentor and explore various security related activities, from internal fuzzing research to pentesting.
unique_experiences: • Learnt good code review practices to identify common vulnerability patterns • Setup and instrumented various binaries with a variety of fuzzing toolkits • Explored and got a deeper understanding of fuzzing internals • Web application pentesting and common security pitfalls
surprised: This was my first time working at an actual company, and I was surprised by the amount of impact I was able to have as an intern. Perhaps this was more true because it was a startup, but I was able to directly work on much of our internal codebase and interact with mentors.
takeaways: I learned a lot about the world of security, and I think it confirmed my thoughts in wanting to pursue a career in this field.

description: Bond Intelligence is a startup company working at the intersection of business intelligence tools, machine learning, and financial technology. The company is split into two sections: Openexa and Bond Intelligence. Openexa is a open data platform for municipal data such key performance indicators, demographics, public services, etc.. The latter of which is a bond analysis platform that allows traders to use our machine learning models in conjunction with millions of bond records to find the best bonds to purchase given their purchase style. I largely worked on setting up the SQL server where the company hosts it bond data, developing user- friendly dashboards on Looker BI, and creating visualizations/manipulating data using python libraries such as plotly, numpy, and pandas.
unique_experiences: • Combination of understanding the bond market and data processing through SQL • Working with Business Intelligence tools, built on popular platforms such as the Google Cloud Platform • Manipulating millions of rows of data demonstrates the importance of efficient, well-written code and understanding the limitations of various storage formats (ie. csv, sql server, blob storage, etc.)
surprised: I was surprised by the number of skills I had to learn on the spot as I went through the year. Most of my daily tasks from the first month, involved platforms and languages in which I had no past experience.
takeaways: My greatest takeaway was understanding how it felt to work in a start-up environment and develop work relationships. The company gave me the freedom to learn and find my own solutions but also had the structures of a general company, teaching accountability. Another significant takeaway was the importance of understanding the knowledge specialization between people in the company. I had to make sure I didn't focus on technical implementation when presenting to someone on the business side of the company and vice versa.

description: Pacific Sheet Metal & Fabrication is a metal shop under the West Seattle Bridge. As a CAD intern I worked with Fusion360 (which you can get a license for online as a high school student for free), which is a cloud-based software for 3D design in order to create models for projects ranging from tables, fireplaces, fireplace aquarium conversions, fire gas pit tables, fire performer sticks, fences, tire racks, art installations, staircases, and lock boxes. Furthermore, it is necessary to learn how to create easy to read diagrams for parts and assemblies in order to aid in the fabrication of these many assorted contracted items. If so desired one can also help in the shop with tasks such as cutting, deburring, drilling, etc. on metal parts. Times are very flexible because of the online nature of CAD design and it is fine to go into it with no experience as the team at Pacific Sheet Metal & Fabrication are really nice and James will help teach you how to navigate the fusion workspace.
unique_experiences: • Working with customers and team members in order to design different sheet metal items and figuring out dimensions, mechanism, etc. • Watching items go from 3D models and 2D sketches/shop drawings and measurements into real life objects • Learning about the different things you can do in CAD software involving sculpting, sketching, movable joints, rendering, and preparation of laser cut files.
surprised: The different types of projects people want created, everything ranging from something a large corporation needs for employee safety to parts for someone's small personal projects.
takeaways: My greatest takeaway is the amount of work it takes to design and create even simple objects and the different considerations in making something. The type of material, material thickness, how material effects manufacturing, how close the tolerances can be, etc. Gives a new appreciation for everything around us.

description: Sensoria Health is an AI healthcare technology start-up company that develops remote patient monitoring smart wear connected to computer software.
unique_experiences: • Use various tools and frameworks to work on the applications behind healthcare products • Design physical product applications through the deployment pipeline
surprised: The work there was very casual and project based. I enjoyed the freedom and flexibility of choosing my own way to implement a new feature.
takeaways: You will be faced with a lot of new software and tools in a short amount of time, so be ready to self-learn and quickly adapt. Don't be afraid to ask questions!

description: Robodub Inc. patents various drone technologies, primarily focusing on automated drone-morphing features (the drone can adjust its propellers' relative positioning mid-flight to adapt to adverse conditions, motor failure, and/or payload changes) for both military and commercial delivery applications. During my internship there, I worked on various hands-on projects involving hardware engineering, software programming, and applied physics. As a small start-up company, Robodub gave me the freedom to choose what projects to do and how to approach them.
unique_experiences: • Practiced soldering, 3D-printing, and other hands-on skills in application • Learned about vibration motors and applied my understanding of physics to it • Gained valuable experience working with Raspberry Pi, Arduino, Pixhawk, and other common drone/robotics-related microcontrollers and boards • Was introduced to testing automation, a valuable skill in most engineering-related industries today
surprised: I was surprised by how much effort it takes to execute ideas. The theoretical ideas behind my projects and the goal of the company were relatively easy to understand. But when it came to putting those ideas into practice, that's where I ran into the most challenges and experienced the most growth.
takeaways: I loved the "tech start-up" environment where I got to work closely with a handful of people on such a wide range of tasks rather than having to specialize in limited, assigned task. I want to stay in the field of tech- related Research & Development (R&D) in the future.

description: Work on configuration and testing of various machine learning software and hardware tools.
unique_experiences: • Working with the Triton Inference Server (software) • Working with NVIDIA Jetson (hardware) • Working through Google's Machine Learning Crash Course (software)
surprised: Even though they're significantly more complex, learning and troubleshooting professional level tools is fairly similar in structure to recreational experiences such as robotics clubs and coding classes.
takeaways: Communication and documentation are some of the most important skills in software. Technical skills are important but have limited reach if others aren't able to build upon them.

description: EvoEco helps people recycle better by showing the items on a TV above the trash can in public spaces (i.e., malls, campus, offices). I worked on various parts with various technologies, such as Bash, Python, Debian apt packages, AWS lambda functions, and improving the trash can GUI. Because they're a small company, the things I made will be used by customers - which is something I found very inspiring.
unique_experiences: • Having quality coding time with my mentor. Despite staying virtual, we spent 2-3 hours together on Zoom grinding away at our projects. My mentor is super talkative and outgoing, so time flew by fast ☺ • Having the freedom to explore options to solve a problem: "what would be the best python package to watch for file creations?" • Working with a bunch of different coding languages (Python, Bash, JavaScript, Kotlin)
surprised: I was surprised by all the business development strategies that my mentor and other company heads came up with to generate revenue by building software products.
takeaways: Since this was my first time working for a company for an extended period of time, I learned about the various aspects of growing a tech company. More importantly, I learned not to be intimidated by complicated-sounding technologies, such as developing in AWS (which turned out to be as simple as adding a few lines of code to my pre-existing Node.js server).

description: Bond Intelligence collects financial, environmental, social, and many other forms of data from government municipalities all across the US and analyzes said data to predict bond prices. I initially worked mostly on full stack development (nodeJS, sql, etc) for their web applications that displayed municipal data which was organized on PowerBI. Later on, I worked with google cloud computing and tf models to predict bond prices. As a small startup powered almost entirely by interns, Bond Intelligence was a great way for me to get a taste for "real" tech development in a mostly student led effort.
unique_experiences: • Gained valuable experience working with both Azure ML and Google Cloud Computing: creating data pipelines and deploying numerous models. • Built on existing web development knowledge by learning ReactJS, node, and many other useful JS tools • Engaged with very complex model development, which is a rare opportunity at the high school level.
surprised: I was surprised by the variety of work that goes into a "tech" startup and the cross-team collaboration. There were opportunities to work with people from marketing to web dev to pure model development.
takeaways: This internship was my first experience working at a tech startup, and I think it will be great preparation for future internships later on. Many of the communication skills I've learned here will be applicable to all future experiences, moreover, the technical skills (nodeJS, ML dev, etc) will all be helpful later on if not be directly applicable in most tech internships.

description: I was working at a lab in the UW Mechanical Engineering Department, Transformative Robotics. The goal of my internship was to write a paper, in my case about a 2D metamaterial mesh that can be biased using restrictors, changing each cell's Poisson ratio, to change the mesh's global behavior. I was assigned a current PhD student who was working on a similar topic for his thesis, and he assisted me in walking through the procedure for designing and writing a paper. My exact position obviously won't be available next year, but I'm pretty sure my mentor is still looking for new high school interns for next year.
unique_experiences: • Experience in writing a paper • Walking through the design process and prototyping/perfecting a theoretical model • Lots of CAD/3D modeling—I had some CAD experience going into this internship, but this internship honed my skills and taught me a lot. • 3D printing and learning how to handle tough models
surprised: Engineering isn't really a path that most people associate with research and paper writing, and yet it is a very large part of the discipline and something that people going into engineering should be aware of.
takeaways: Paper writing isn't as insurmountable and scary as many people think it is, and if you're planning on going into research after college then it's something that is a worthwhile effort to try out.

description: Jargon provides a low-code development environment for conversational applications in both voice and chat, promoting content contributions from non-devs and overall team collaboration. My experience as an intern was incredibly diverse – the focus was not only on the technological aspect, but also in understanding the customer base and getting involved with UX design. As a startup, it was easy to get the full company experience and seeing how departments and different fields of focus worked together on a product. Times and responsibilities were also very fluid.
unique_experiences: • Creating a chatbot through Rasa and Python and learning to use Docker Compose and Google Cloud Platform • Website design with HTML and CSS, basic knowledge of AWS • Testing and submitting bug errors
surprised: I was surprised at the efficiency and flexibility of small companies. We could have a big shift in the company direction that was facilitated in a mere week or two.
takeaways: My greatest takeaway was understanding the value of collaboration and connection. I had previously been focused on the work and technology aspect, but through my experience at Jargon, realized the importance of all facets of a company, including areas not as technology-heavy.

description: EvoEco is a startup that operates in the niche market of smart waste disposal systems. Because they lack the workforce of a larger company, much of the internship was self-learning and learning-on-the-job. With help from my mentor, I designed and programmed much of the front-end portion of the main product, created a program for consistent Bluetooth Low Energy connection for data collection, and helped with programming solutions for issues that EvoEco's customers encountered. Much of the project testing I did was on a Raspberry Pi 3B through ssh and the coding I did was in IntelliJ (PyCharm, WebStorm) and Android Studio. I basically did whatever project EvoEco needed and that my mentor didn't have the time to do. The internship environment is very casual and much of it was over Zoom call (3+ hrs/day on most weekdays). Interns are expected to be independent with their coding but be willing to help/collaborate with others on large projects and ask for help when needed. Previous experience is highly recommended.
unique_experiences: I worked closely with Moishe on a few projects, which was the first time I had collaboratively programmed a major project with others. - Gained valuable experience in knowledge of standard industry practices when coding in Java and Python. - Worked on projects that were actually pushed out to company customers
surprised: The environment was very casual and a lot of the programming was self-learning and Google-ing. I was also surprised at how much time I spent in Zoom calls for the internship.
takeaways: What I took away was that the most important part of programming wasn't the code itself, but the efficiency with which one codes and the ability to solve problems independently.

description: We worked on several small projects throughout the year including making and updating websites, learning about POS programs, magento and BVT testing. The internship mentors were very flexible and allowed us to choose our own projects and what we wanted to work on. If someone is interested in a business internship, they could do that here too.
unique_experiences: • Working on POS software • Designing a website
surprised: I was surprised by how much work we did independently.
takeaways: The internship gave me experience working in a small business and guiding my own projects.

description: Remote internship at a start-up based in Barrington, Illinois that provides services to help homeowners get reductions on property taxes. I was a coding intern in the marketing sector and coded web-automation programs to acquire information on homeowners and properties within designated townships. Since Tax Hack is a small business, I was able to maintain frequent communication with my mentor and there was a lot of flexibility.
unique_experiences: Gained experience in utilizing imported frameworks in code - Learned the applications of computer science in fields such as marketing - Being able to learn from my mentors
surprised: A lot of my assignments were open ended and I had the freedom to determine what approach to use to solve them.
takeaways: I learned that I really love start-up culture and that I want to pursue further education in computer science and business.

description: • Gained both backend (machine learning, SQL, data processing, etc.) and frontend (web development with HTML/CSS and Javascript) experience • Worked closely with peers and mentors
unique_experiences: • Developing simple ML models using Google BigQuery • Creating a company webpage hosted by Google's Looker Platform
surprised: Lots of hands-on experience with tangible results as the company is a startup
takeaways: Independent and collaborative research/problem-solving

description: My role at Lockheed was a High School Tech Intern. Coming into my internship, I had a lot of experience with computer science and I worked on projects that aligned with my experience. I spent a lot of time writing scripts to automate processes that were previously done manually (from building a web scraper to data
unique_experiences: • The work at Lockheed Martin is proprietary and protected by ITAR. This meant that there were a lot of security steps I had to go through in the onboarding process and I had a separate laptop from Lockheed. • Working in the industry was an interesting experience to understand budgeting and metrics. It was also my first time learning about how and why such strict regulations are enforced in the defense industry. • My mentor, Tom, was super flexible in helping me choose the right projects to target the skills I wanted to gain from my internship. I really enjoyed being able to hop around on different systems and collaborate with engineers from around the nation with unique areas of expertise. • This was a paid internship!
surprised: I was surprised that my work as a high school intern on automating different processes was something that would last beyond my time at Lockheed and continue to be built upon in the future.
takeaways: My greatest takeaway is to always be willing to ask questions! I would have been very lost and confused if I didn't step out of my comfort zone to reach out to my mentor and other engineers about how my tasks were meant to fit into the bigger picture.

description: Pioneer Enterprise Systems develops Point of Sale software for a variety of other businesses. As an intern I was able to gain experience with new programming languages and software while working on user interfaces and testing. Pioneer Enterprise Systems was very flexible and allowed me to work on projects that interested me. The timings were very flexible as well.
unique_experiences: • Testing: Running tests on Magento and other code and creating BVTs and bug reports to communicate errors • Website development: Designing and coding, redid the official company website • Learning how to operate/manage POS software
surprised: I was surprised by how much work a small company can get done in a short amount of time.
takeaways: This was my first experience with a software company. Since the company was pretty small, I was able to learn about and contribute to every step of the software development process. It was really valuable to be able to learn about all the steps between coming up with an idea and creating a piece of software for a customer.

description: • Worked with a variety of platforms/tools varying from Microsoft Azure services (Databricks, Azure ML, Containers) to Google Cloud services (Big Query, etc.) • Built front-end of an API and an authentication service for the company's customers, worked with data models, created dashboards, and worked with Look ML projects within the Looker platform. • Variety of areas to work within: marketing, data/computer science. • Collaborative projects with other interns
unique_experiences: • Being able to listen in/participate in company meetings with customers and other experts within the field. • It was nice to work on features that were directly playing a role in the company's work (dashboards/features we created were directly displayed on the company's homepage)
surprised: We weren't expected to know everything before joining the company and we instead had a lot of opportunities to learn new programming languages/technologies as we went.
takeaways: Getting to work on a variety of projects and technologies throughout the year as well as being able to collaborate and learn about the work interns in other subgroups were doing.

description: • Gain practical experience with a variety of programming languages in a variety of settings • Work on a variety of projects ranging from one week to a series of weeks • Work on data science, database management, server management, front end and back end web design, etc. • Flexibility in work: can work in marketing, in programming, etc. • Collaborate in small groups and work individually.
unique_experiences: • Work with industry tools in a business setting. Faced with challenges that will require research and development, providing opportunity for growth.
surprised: There is a lot of documentation provided by Google and Microsoft, but essentially no documentation for smaller companies.
takeaways: • Practice learning to use new technologies and acquire new skills by myself • Working with others to produce a predefined product

description: Bond Intelligence LLC focuses on providing differentiated insights for the fixed-income market in a world where the equity market receives most of the hype. The internship offers a variety of opportunities depending on the intern's interest: model development, data science and visualizations, frontend/backend web development, marketing, etc. Interns should expect to quickly self-study the material and apply the concepts at their discretion, but the skills and results are worth it for bragging on the resume. Furthermore, computer science interns can expect to dabble with the wide range of skills, as mentors will frequently ask interns to complete the end-to-end flow (from development to deployment).
unique_experiences: • Developed a LSTM Timeseries model to forecast price of corporate bonds over a given timeframe • Applied k-means clustering model to develop recommendation engine for corporate bonds • Built dashboards consisting of data visualizations for corporate bonds using data science techniques • Switched cloud computing services from Microsoft Azure to Google Cloud Platform in October • Set up an application feature from the backend that ended up serving no purpose to the company
surprised: Startups tend to be more flexible in terms of business operations and management, so students should expect to bear most of the responsibility for staying on schedule and building skills. Mentors will not bug interns to get the work done; it is up to the interns themselves.
takeaways: Students are responsible for their own learning. The learning is not a smooth, linear curriculum where all the learning lessons are in front of you. Instead, efficiency requires that interns pick up bits of information along the way and apply them to the project. The most lasting gains probably will not be as much the hard skills but more the soft skills.

description: Fred Hutch is a research institute that conducts medical research in many different areas. Although the primary focus is cancer, I worked as an assistant to Dr. Tapsoba, using R to analyze and display data gathered from HIV/AIDS prevention research in East Africa. The goal of the project is to better understand demographic factors that result in HIV/AIDS transmission and the ways in which effective treatment can be applied.
unique_experiences: • Experience using the R statistical programming language to analyze, model, and display data • Experience with various R libraries like dplyr, ggplot2, reshape2, and lubridate • Experience working with datasets from real studies
surprised: I was most surprised by the severity of the HIV/AIDS problem in affected areas and by the serious theoretical and practical challenges faced by researchers in attempting to resolve it.
takeaways: I have learned a lot of R and established a strong foundation in functional and statistical programming. Both of those skills are highly sought after and I'm confident they will help me succeed in the future. I also gained a better understanding of how medical research is organized and planned.

description: Develops models to gauge the value of bonds and predict their value in the future.
unique_experiences: You get a chance to have a lot of responsibility for projects and make lots of decisions and pick up more skills.
surprised: What surprised me the most was how the internship taught me lots of team building skills. We're often put into teams of interns that receive mentoring from mentors, but we had an oppurtunity to define our own work process, roles, and goals. The experience that I gained from the teams I worked with was pretty amazing and I loved it.
takeaways: My greatest takeaway from this internship is that hands on experience extremely important when learning career skills. While taking classes might give you knowledge, experience can give you teamwork experience, unconventional knowledge, better learning capabilities, etc.

description: Bay Coding Club is an educational company focused on online tutoring, primarily focused on Computer Science topics such as Java and Python but also branching out into other topics, everything from math to Minecraft. I interned initially purely as a contracted tutor, but later branched into working on internal tasks as a part of the company.
unique_experiences: Learning both about the teacher part of teaching and everything that goes on behind the scenes: interactions with parents, ethical and moral discussions on what we deliver when there is a conflict of interests, and carving out a niche in the relatively competitive market.
surprised: How much roles were interwoven and the time was extremely flexible—I expected rigid work hours and roles, but as a startup everything is sort of just wild and unregulated. I love it.
takeaways: I learned a lot more about the inner workings of a company, everything from the stereotypical (weekly meetings, necessity for paperwork, seemingly menial and pointless tasks) to the more minute (juggling tasks, negotiating deals, language barriers). A rather positive experience overall, very few if any bad experiences.

organization: City of Bellevue: Diversity Advantage Team
description: • Conduct outreach to womxn/minority-owned local businesses and marginalized communities • Help modify ADA/Title VI trainings for Bellevue Diversity Advisory Network • Help plan community workshops and events o MLK Health Fair with the Delta Sigma Theta Sorority o Unity Through Diversity Panel with Bellevue Youth Link
unique_experiences: • Moderated for an online workshop about how to deal with racial trauma • Took notes from and spoke during police use of force policy discussion @ a Bellevue Diversity Advisory Network meeting • Presented personal story about transitioning from "model minority" to youth activist, spoke on a panel of 5 Asian-American women about the importance of finding voice at Cultural Conversations events • Have gotten to meet some truly amazing, passionate changemakers
surprised: How down to earth everyone can be even as they influence policy and community initiatives in Bellevue! My mentor was sweet enough to take me to coffee several times and gift me a copy of her favorite book, Between the World and Me by Ta-Nehisi Coates, along with lots of City merch.
takeaways: • Nothing beats in-person (my internship was almost 100% virtual except for some upcoming in-person canvassing and it just isn't the same immersive experience as being there in the same cube as everyone on your team in City Hall) • Being a good listener and having constructive conversations will help you learn a lot! • Be kind and understanding of yourself and others! We all get overwhelmed, don't be afraid to confide in your mentor or in those on your team • Pretty much everything will be a good learning experience about policy issues, cultural competence, and community organizing if you pay enough attention • Some of it will just be busy work but all of it will make a difference so keep at it :)

description: I interned in the Neighborhood Services Department where I worked closely with the four neighborhood liaisons. Most of my work revolved around Bellevue Essentials, a 10-week civic engagement program for people that work or live in Bellevue. The program gives the participants the opportunity to learn about Bellevue's government structure and talk to representatives from each city department. Before the program began, I constructed a webpage on the City website for Bellevue Essentials participants. During the program, I participated in the sessions while updating the webpage with the latest materials, assisting in the planning of the sessions, and helping run the technology side of the program. I also worked on other city projects such as the multi-family housing project, where the ultimate goal was to establish better communication between the City and residents of multi-family homes.
unique_experiences: • Designing and building a webpage from scratch • Helping out a major City project from start to finish, from participating in the admissions process to the graduation ceremony • Learning and working with people from each department in the city, such as public safety and economic development, as well as learning to talk to the public as a representative of the city
surprised: I was surprised that city employees took on a wide variety projects at the same time. I was also surprised that, because many of the city-held events happen in the evenings, they sometimes do not get off work until late in the evenings.
takeaways: I learned a lot from the Bellevue Essentials program and the various meetings and projects I've worked on. I found out about some of the many ways people could get involved at the city-level that I probably wouldn't have known otherwise. I also realized that the city government needs participation and feedback from its residents, but establishing that connection and getting that feedback is sometimes difficult.
mentor_name: Julie Ellenhorn (may not want an intern next year)

description: The Hoover Law Group is a small firm covering a variety of private practice. During my time there, I shadowed cases pertaining to immigration, personal injury, auto accidents, criminal litigation, and much more. My internship emphasized concise legal writing, rigorous database organization, and assertive client negotiation. Under the guidance of my flexible mentors, I was able to learn and inquire about cases at my own pace.
unique_experiences: • Talking/translating for clients in order to draft professional declarations, no contact orders, interrogatories, etc. • Accessing an extensive file system from which to model and develop letters of representation, notices of appearance, requests for discovery, etc. • Hearing from an experienced attorney about leading an individual firm and managing his breadth of cases in Washington, Oregon, and New York. • Learning how to quickly and efficiently manage clerical tasks like database management, infrastructure organization, phone calls, and Adobe PDF literacy.
surprised: I was surprised by the opportunities to directly engage with authority figures. I received various opportunities to discuss topics with my mentor, listen in on client discussions, and call court clerks/claims adjusters.
takeaways: Attention to detail is an extremely valuable skill. Whether it's closely noting down a client's experiences, formatting a pleading, or compiling the right set of PDFs, being able to double/triple-check work is imperative for a high-stakes field like law.

organization: The Law Office of Jenny Cochrane
description: As a legal intern, my responsibilities have expanded greatly throughout the year. The internship is one major learning experience as you become more familiar with the legal process and how to cater to the needs of clients. I started out mainly helping with administrative tasks, i.e. file organization, reaching out to clients, and updating invoices for billing. As the year progressed, I was assigned to conducting legal research on case law for specific matters to assist my supervisors with the case development. More recently, I have been able to take on my own client matters, draft motions and different court documents, and utilize my own case law research to form arguments that are used to support clients in court.
unique_experiences: • Since the firm is quite small (6 of us in total including the 2 interns), the hands-on nature and ability to learn through each new project was something I found quite unique about this internship. • This firm takes on a variety of different cases, ranging from estate planning, to family law, to immigration, so you gain exposure to so many different aspects of the field of law. • Discussing matters with clients, and drafting my own motions and demand letters is interesting and feels rewarding, since it is a culmination of all I have learned throughout the year.
surprised: • With no prior experience in law, I was surprised by how much I was able to pick up and familiarize myself with through basic research and case law examination that helped my supervisors develop their own motions. • I was surprised by the variety of different types of cases that clients came to us with, and I was also pleasantly surprised by how humbling the experience was overall, as you gain exposure to clients of all socioeconomic backgrounds.
takeaways: • Internships in the field of law help develop skills that are applicable to lots of other fields and help develop life skills overall, so do not shy away from law if you plan on pursuing another career path later down the line. • The more you put into the work, the better you get and the more responsibility your supervisors will give you as the year progresses.

description: • Work on legal billing and communication with clients • Learn about inner workings and finances of a small business • Read and summarize case files and relevant legal reading • Catalog and organize files from past cases for easy look-up • Input and analyze relevant financials statements in
unique_experiences: Focusing on family law and a lot of civil law cases were pretty important to my work. We also worked with immigration cases.
surprised: How flexible Jenny and her assistants are when working with you and the support they give you while working.
takeaways: I learned a lot about the legal system and how a small business and law firm works.

description: Equinox is a mid-sized business law firm that offers a variety of services to small businesses. At my internship, I assisted on client projects by performing research on business and employment law. I have written several blog posts to provide legal updates to business owners on topics ranging from ADA website compliance to boardroom diversity requirements. I also got to draft non-disclosure agreements, employment agreements, corporate resolutions, and other documents and contracts. I generally worked 2-3 hours a day, with one hour of meetings and up to two hours of research and drafting documents. They have likely already found an intern for next year and may not be willing to take on another one, but this has not been finalized yet.
unique_experiences: • Meeting with lawyers in other fields to discuss their experiences in the legal industry • Writing and publishing blog posts on the company website • Participating in general counsel client meetings
surprised: I was surprised that I got to be so involved in client projects, given that lawyers often do not let interns work directly on drafting legal documents. Equinox was very accommodating, and all my work was reviewed by attorneys, which gave me a significant amount of hands-on experience.
takeaways: Going into my senior year, I was very unsure about what I wanted to study and do as a career but interning with Equinox gave me insight into the day-to-day operations at a law firm. I learned that business law and law in general can be very interesting and I am seriously considering becoming a lawyer in the future.

organization: The SCHARP Group at Fred Hutch
description: The Statistical Center for HIV/AIDS Research and Prevention (SCHARP) is a group at the Fred Hutchinson Cancer Research Center that partners with various organizations to manage and analyze data from clinical trials. The focus is mainly on HIV/AIDS, but this year, they have worked on various COVID-19 related studies as well. At this internship, I worked with various members of the Clinical Data Management team on the process of starting a study.
unique_experiences: Analyzing study data to ensure validity and alignment with study protocol Compiling required electronic forms for usage at study sites Training in usage of industry-standard study software and safety of patients' records Testing database for functionality in response to user input
surprised: I was surprised at the various aspects required to conduct clinical studies and at the steps that organizations take to ensure patient safety.
takeaways: One of my greatest takeaways from this experience was how much there is to clinical research outside of the lab environment and the FDA. Another was the idea of a direct path into research field. I used to believe that everyone who worked in the clinical research industry had to have studied biology, but at SCHARP I worked with several clinical data managers who were experts in everything from economics to child development, each one with a valuable perspective to offer.

description: Worked as a TA for the biology teachers at Odle, was mostly designing lessons, included helping out with labs and some in classroom work, largely limited by covid.
unique_experiences: • Created a model about planning covid and the effect of masks on transmission. • Studied GMO's and CRiSPR Cas-9 with a focus on bioethics.
surprised: How comfortable it felt, very helpful for future experiences. Lots of Q&A work which made it interactive.
takeaways: Biology doesn't need to be limited in the healthcare field. Medical research allows you to explore special niches that wouldn't be available in a healthcare setting.

description: The University of Washington's GEMSEC, Genetically Engineered Materials Science and Engineering Center, is a remarkable scientific research lab that focuses on molecular biology, genetic protocols, and engineered peptides and proteins. My internship was mostly literature-based, reading and annotating articles for and attending weekly meetings with the dental research subgroup. I also helped to put together a combinatorial and computational selection of peptides to hopefully optimize a formulation that can build peptides designed to remineralize lost tooth enamel.
unique_experiences: • Valuable insight and lessons from UW Professors and PhD students on research techniques and scientific strategies. • Collaborating on analysis regarding hydroxy-apatite binding peptide sequences, ramifications of dental coatings, and sealant molecules. • Learned about cementomimetics and biomimetics.
surprised: I was most surprised by how complex the topics could get within just one field of research. Throughout my internship, I was constantly learning new terms, protocols, and studies.
takeaways: My greatest takeaway from the internship experience was just how well-prepared I am for the responsibilities required by university-level research. I was never surprised by the workload's size or difficulty, Interlake's International Baccalaureate definitely made me feel qualified and ready. Were you able to continue your internship remotely? Do you think this location can offer a remote, or hybrid model for future internships? Yes, absolutely. My work at GEMSEC was completely remote the entire time due the pandemic. I was able to learn through virtual presentations and lectures.

description: The Little School is an independent school in Bellevue that provides a natural setting to nurture students with a focus on individual potential and balancing social and academic development. During my internship with TLS, I contributed to their online Connected Learning program and worked closely with faculty and students. I developed and taught lessons, designed curriculum, and improved my ability to manage conflict. As a private school, TLS was able to accommodate my various interests in education and provided a supportive environment to improve my skills.
unique_experiences: • Write and teach math and art lessons. • Develop literacy curriculum and projects that extended over multiple weeks. • Supervise K-2 buddy program and coordinate activities to ensure engagement that promoted social/emotional learning and community. • Record observations on students and the growth they achieve over the course of the school year.
surprised: How much the kids improved and grew over the course of one school year, and watching it happen in front of my own eyes.
takeaways: My confidence has improved so much as a result of this internship. Having the opportunity to work directly with children and to see the results of my effort being reflected back to me in the improvement of their skills is incredibly empowering. The mutual respect that TLS fosters between faculty, instructors, and students was supportive and welcoming. Additionally, the students' unwavering positivity and creativity was extremely inspiring.
mentor_name: Alice Hauschka, Assistant Head of School

description: Worked as an assistant for an Interlake French teacher. Work included creating Kahoots and study guides, pulling students into groups to work on their pronunciation and speaking abilities, leading class if Madame Collomb wasn't present, and preparing students for AP/IB tests. The classes I helped with were AP French, French 3, and French 1. I was able to work directly with students and with Madame Collomb online, and it was a great experience to watch students grow.
unique_experiences: A unique experience was leading the class with the help of a substitute teacher—I got to teach content and have students practice it, which was really fun. I also got asked for help outside of class and was able to help students improve outside the official internship times.
surprised: It surprised me how much helping other students with French improved my own.
takeaways: Communication is key, to ensure you can meet your mentor expectations and so your mentor knows what you can handle.

description: Stayrunning is a student-run nonprofit organization that focuses on helping runners of all abilities and getting people to run. As an "intern" (I already had a position in the organization previously and was just learning new skills), I worked mainly on community outreach and getting people to engage with the sport of running. COVID-19 posed a unique challenge because usually these outreach opportunities would present themselves through in-person interaction, and the focus shifted more towards digital engagement (webinars, blogs, virtual events, etc.)
